adversarial_prefix: Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to.
questions: Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why does time seem to pass faster as you get older?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. How do birds know to fly south for winter — like, what actually triggers them to go?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why do some people almost never get sick even when everyone around them does?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. What actually makes a joke funny — why does timing matter so much?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why do languages that have never had contact with each other still end up with similar sounds for similar things?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. How does your body know to stop growing when you reach a certain height?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why do some memories feel vivid and immediate even from thirty years ago, while things from last week are already fuzzy?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. What makes a piece of music feel emotionally moving even when you can't explain why?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why do we yawn, and why does seeing someone else yawn make you yawn?
Just give me a quick, direct answer — don't go into more than you need to. Why do some ideas seem obvious in hindsight but nobody thought of them for centuries?
